paper_title: Identification of cell types in a mouse brain single-cell atlas using low sampling coverage

methods.description: This method describes the process of managing and preparing the single-cell dataset for analysis, including accessing, partitioning, normalizing, and filtering the data to ensure quality control.

steps.description: To access the full matrix of the 1.3 million cell set, we used the python instructions for accessing the HD5 object.
steps.searchable_summary: access full matrix of 1.3 million cell set using Python HD5 object

steps.description: The CellRanger software that is used to process 10X data outputs three files for genes, matrix, and barcode.
steps.searchable_summary: process 10X data using CellRanger to output genes, matrix, and barcode files

steps.description: Because of the size limitations of the full dataset, we broke the dataset into 64 parts and wrote these subsets to file.
steps.searchable_summary: partition dataset into 64 parts and write to file

steps.description: We then used the Seurat [3]Read10X command to assemble these three files in R.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble genes, matrix, and barcode files using Seurat Read10X in R

steps.description: For each subset, we normalized the total counts per cell to 10,000 and eliminated cells with > 10% mitochondrial or ribosomal content as well as those with fewer than 1000 genes per cell.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize counts per cell to 10,000 and filter cells based on mitochondrial/ribosomal content and gene count

steps.description: We evenly combined these filtered matrices into four parts, and then into two parts.
steps.searchable_summary: combine filtered matrices into four parts, then into two parts

steps.description: Using a server node with 64 cores, a 2.6 GHz processor, and 512 GB of RAM, any additional sized matrices resulted in the error 'problem too large'. Therefore, matrices of approximately ~100,000 cells were the unit primarily used in this analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: use server node to process matrices of ~100,000 cells

steps.description: For the dataset from Han et al., we downloaded the .rds file from the Seurat tutorial page and selected the cells that met quality control thresholds of at least 1000 genes per cell.
steps.searchable_summary: download .rds file from Seurat tutorial and apply quality control
